title: Model Routing by Task Phase
concept: Using small, cheap models for simple task steps (routing/validation) and only calling frontier models for reasoning.

exercises.prompt: What can you do to speed up processes in agentic loops?

options.text: Use smaller models for every step
Combine multiple tasks in a single call
Use small, cheap models for simple tasks and reserve bigger models for reasoning

exercises.explain: Using small, cheap models for straightforward tasks and saving larger models for complex reasoning speeds up processes.

cards.title: What is Model Routing?
cards.body: Model routing means choosing the right AI model based on the task you need to do. Some tasks can use simple models, while complex ones need advanced models.

cards.title: Model Routing by Task Phase
cards.body: This concept involves using cheaper, smaller models for easy tasks (like validation) and utilizing larger, more powerful models only when deep reasoning is necessary.

cards.title: Like Choosing Cars for the Journey
cards.body: Imagine choosing a bicycle for a short trip around the block and a sports car for a long road trip. You use less energy and time when picking the vehicle that fits the journey.

cards.title: Practical Application
cards.body: For a task like checking grammar, a small model is fine. For understanding a complex legal argument, you would use a larger model.

cards.title: Myth: Bigger Models Are Always Better
cards.body: Just because a model is bigger doesn't mean it's the right choice for every task. Smaller models can handle simple tasks more efficiently.

cards.title: Model Size vs. Task Complexity
cards.body: Not every task needs a heavyweight model; choose wisely!

exercises.prompt: Which model should you use for checking spelling?
exercises.explain: Checking spelling is a simple task, so a smaller, cheaper model is more efficient.

exercises.prompt: It is always better to use the largest model for every task.
exercises.explain: False. It's often better to use smaller models for basic tasks and save larger models for complex reasoning.

exercises.prompt: Choose the correct word: For simple tasks, use small models, and for reasoning, use ___ models.
exercises.sentence: For simple tasks, use small models, and for reasoning, use large models.
exercises.explain: The correct term is 'large'. We want smaller models for simple tasks and larger models when deeper reasoning is needed.

exercises.prompt: Which statement is generated by an AI?

options.text: Always use large models for all tasks to ensure quality.
Using small models can often speed up processes in agentic loops.
Small models are never effective regardless of the task.

exercises.explain: The AI-generated statement points out that using small models can speed up processes, highlighting efficient model routing.

exercises.prompt: When should you prioritize using a small model over a larger one?

options.text: When the task requires simple checks or validations
When the task is very complex and needs deep understanding
When you want to impress someone with a big model
When the task can be done by any model

exercises.explain: You should prioritize using a small model for tasks that involve simple checks or validations because they are quicker and cheaper for those tasks.
